raw.population: N:62 Sex: - Age: 8 month- 6 yrs WT: -
raw.objective: “To evaluate PPK and the relationship between plasma concentration and DDPVA in a homogeneous group of children with epilepsy”
raw.observations: Model limited to estimate VPA CL in children of 8-72 months. The model describes an asymptotic relation of increased CL as age increases. A linear correlation is found between VPA plasma level and dose administered. Results show show that dose required to reach mean plasma [VPA] decreases with age. A level:dose ratio should be used with care as the inter-individual variation in children <2 yrs is 50%.
raw.dose: Steady state, oral doses in patients
raw.equations: if(AGE<24) CL = (((theta1/1000)*WT )*((AGE/12)**theta2))*exp(eta1) if(AGE>24 & AGE<48) CL = (((theta1/1000)*WT )*((AGE/12)**theta2))*exp(eta2) if(AGE>48) CL = (((theta1/1000)*WT )*((AGE/12)**theta2))*exp(eta3) Cipred=DOSE/(CL*Tau) Cobs=Cipred #no error model reported DOSE is VPA dose in mg Tau is dosing interval in hours AGE: in months CL is converted from mL/kg/h to L/h by dividing by 1000*WT

normalization.omega: Values labelled OMEGA in the appendix are retained as NONMEM variances; no CV reconversion is applied.
normalization.eta: ETA distribution is inferred only when the appendix equation explicitly uses exp(ETA) or additive ETA.
normalization.source_text: The appendix row is preserved verbatim in raw; normalized fields never replace it.
